metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Dance Technique and Performance 11 advances students' technical skill, performance quality, and movement vocabulary in one or more dance disciplines. Students deepen their understanding of the body as an expressive instrument, explore dance repertoire from historical and contemporary perspectives, and engage in increasingly sophisticated performance and rehearsal processes. First Peoples dance knowledge and diverse global traditions are studied with cultural respect. This course prepares students for Dance Technique and Performance 12.
metadata.general_description: An advanced senior dance course developing refined technique, expressive range, performance quality, and choreographic understanding — with an emphasis on historical and cultural dance contexts and preparation for Dance 12 performance.

big_ideas.statement: Technical mastery in dance enables greater expressive freedom and artistic communication.
big_ideas.elaboration: As dancers develop technical precision and physical control, they gain greater capacity for expressive nuance — their bodies become more capable of communicating the subtleties of emotion, story, and meaning.

big_ideas.statement: Individual and collective expression is rooted in history, culture, community, and values.
big_ideas.elaboration: All dance traditions carry cultural memory — they reflect the values, histories, and identities of the communities that created them. Understanding this context is essential to authentic interpretation and performance.

big_ideas.statement: Growth as a dancer requires sustained practice, critical self-reflection, and artistic risk.
big_ideas.elaboration: Becoming a skilled dancer demands long-term commitment, the courage to confront limitations, and the willingness to be vulnerable in performance and creation.

big_ideas.statement: Dance reflects and shapes cultural values and social understanding.
big_ideas.elaboration: Dance has always been a vehicle for cultural transmission, social commentary, and community celebration. Dancers are cultural agents whose choices of form, style, and content carry meaning.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate refined dance technique and elements in multiple styles; develop performance quality and expressive range; create choreographic works using complex devices and structures; take purposeful creative risks.
i_can_statements: I can demonstrate refined technique, dynamic range, and spatial precision in multiple dance styles.
I can develop and expand my performance quality — presence, projection, and expressive commitment.
I can create choreographic works using complex choreographic devices and formal structures.
I can take purposeful creative risks in performance and composition, with awareness of artistic intent.

competencies.raw: Analyze and evaluate dance works using advanced dance vocabulary; apply feedback to refine artistic work; examine the relationship between dance and its cultural and historical context.
i_can_statements: I can analyze and evaluate dance works — including my own — using advanced technical and aesthetic vocabulary.
I can apply detailed feedback to make specific technical and expressive improvements.
I can examine the historical, cultural, and social contexts that shape the dance forms I study and perform.

competencies.raw: Perform in varied contexts with technical precision and artistic expression; document and share creative and performance work; use dance to communicate personal and social ideas.
i_can_statements: I can perform prepared and improvisational dance work in varied contexts with technical precision and expressive commitment.
I can document my artistic development through portfolios, video analysis, and reflective writing.
I can use dance to communicate complex personal, social, and cultural ideas to an audience.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate professionalism in rehearsal and performance contexts; engage with First Peoples dance knowledge; examine dance's role in social change and cultural transmission.
i_can_statements: I can demonstrate consistent professionalism — punctuality, preparation, and ensemble commitment — in rehearsal and performance.
I can engage with First Peoples dance knowledge with cultural respect, understanding protocols and significance.
I can examine and articulate how dance transmits cultural knowledge and contributes to social change.

areas.name: Advanced Technique
areas.raw: Advanced technique in one or more dance disciplines.
topics: Style-specific technical training: ballet, contemporary, jazz, hip hop, or cultural forms
Alignment and anatomy: deepened understanding for injury prevention and efficiency
Strength and conditioning specific to dance demands
Advanced combinations: complex footwork, turns, jumps, and floor work
Dynamic range: contrast, suspension, impulse, and quality of movement

areas.name: Repertoire and Performance
topics: Learning and performing set repertoire: historically and culturally diverse works
Rehearsal process: note-taking, self-correction, and ensemble sensitivity
Stage production: lighting, sound, costume, and technical elements
Performance analysis: watching and critiquing professional dance works
Post-performance reflection and growth planning

areas.name: Choreographic Development
topics: Complex choreographic structures: through-composed, episodic, and abstract forms
Site-specific and interdisciplinary choreography
Collaborative choreography: shared vision and ensemble creation
Creating movement to address social or cultural issues
Documenting choreography: notation, video, and score development

areas.name: Dance History and Cultural Contexts
areas.raw: Diverse dance histories, First Peoples traditions, and dance in social context.
topics: Western dance history: court dance to contemporary — key figures and movements
First Peoples dance traditions: regional diversity, protocols, and contemporary First Peoples dance artists
Global dance traditions: Bharatanatyam, African contemporary, Latin forms, and others
Dance and social change: how dance has reflected and driven social movements
Ethics of cultural representation in dance casting and programming
